question: In Adobe Character Animator, how do you set the scene duration to match the current playhead position?
answer: Right-click on the timeline at the desired location to open the context menu, then select 'Set Scene Duration to Playhead' to adjust the scene duration to the playhead's current position.
keypoints: The user right-clicks on the timeline at a specific point, causing a context menu to appear with options such as 'Set Work Area Start to Playhead,' 'Set Work Area End to Playhead,' and 'Set Scene Duration to Playhead.'
The user selects the 'Set Scene Duration to Playhead' option from the context menu, which adjusts the scene duration to match the current playhead position.

question: In Adobe Character Animator, how do you trim the scene to the work area and clear timeline selections?
answer: Select 'Trim Scene to Work Area' from the context menu to fit the scene within the work area boundaries, then click 'Clear' to remove any selected elements or settings.
keypoints: Next, the user chooses the 'Trim Scene to Work Area' option from the context menu, trimming the scene to fit within the defined work area boundaries.
Finally, the user clicks on the 'Clear' option in the context menu to remove any selected elements or settings from the timeline.

question: In Adobe Character Animator, what steps are needed to set the scene duration to the playhead and advance the work area?
answer: After right-clicking the timeline to open the context menu, select 'Set Scene Duration to Playhead' to match the playhead position. Then click 'Advance Work Area' in the same menu to move the work area forward by one frame.
keypoints: The user right-clicks on the timeline at a specific point, causing a context menu to appear with options such as 'Set Work Area Start to Playhead,' 'Set Work Area End to Playhead,' and 'Set Scene Duration to Playhead.'
The user selects the 'Set Scene Duration to Playhead' option from the context menu, which adjusts the scene duration to match the current playhead position.
After setting the scene duration, the user clicks on the 'Advance Work Area' option in the same context menu, moving the work area forward by one frame.

question: In Adobe Character Animator, how do you navigate to the work area start, trim the scene, and clear selections?
answer: Select 'Go to Work Area Start' from the context menu to move the playhead to the beginning of the work area, then choose 'Trim Scene to Work Area' to fit the scene within the boundaries. Finally, click 'Clear' to remove any selected elements.
keypoints: The user then selects the 'Go to Work Area Start' option from the context menu, which moves the playhead back to the beginning of the work area.
Next, the user chooses the 'Trim Scene to Work Area' option from the context menu, trimming the scene to fit within the defined work area boundaries.
Finally, the user clicks on the 'Clear' option in the context menu to remove any selected elements or settings from the timeline.

question: In Adobe Character Animator, what is the complete process to set scene duration, advance the work area, and navigate to the work area start?
answer: Right-click the timeline to open the context menu and select 'Set Scene Duration to Playhead' to match the playhead position. Click 'Advance Work Area' to move the work area forward by one frame, then select 'Go to Work Area Start' to reposition the playhead at the work area beginning.
keypoints: The user right-clicks on the timeline at a specific point, causing a context menu to appear with options such as 'Set Work Area Start to Playhead,' 'Set Work Area End to Playhead,' and 'Set Scene Duration to Playhead.'
The user selects the 'Set Scene Duration to Playhead' option from the context menu, which adjusts the scene duration to match the current playhead position.
After setting the scene duration, the user clicks on the 'Advance Work Area' option in the same context menu, moving the work area forward by one frame.
The user then selects the 'Go to Work Area Start' option from the context menu, which moves the playhead back to the beginning of the work area.
